## What is QD OLED monitor?

A QD OLED monitor is a cutting-edge display technology that combines Quantum Dot and OLED (Organic Light Emitting Diode) technologies. Quantum Dots, which are nanocrystals, enhance color accuracy and brightness by converting blue OLED light into highly saturated red and green light. This results in stunning visuals with vibrant colors, deep blacks, and high contrast ratios. QD OLED monitors offer an exceptional viewing experience, making them ideal for various applications, including gaming, photo editing, and multimedia consumption.

## What are the advantages of a QD OLED monitor?

The advantages of a Quantum Dot Organic Light Emitting Diode (QD OLED)  monitor are many. You get exceptional color accuracy, high contrast ratios, deep blacks, wide viewing angles, and energy efficiency. Compared to traditional LCD (Liquid Crystal Display) monitors, QD OLED displays offer a superior visual experience, making them perfect for tasks like professional photo and video editing, gaming, and general multimedia consumption.

## What is the role of Quantum Dots in a QD OLED monitor?

Quantum Dots play a crucial role in QD OLED monitors by enhancing color accuracy and purity. These nanocrystals act as a color filter, converting blue OLED light into highly saturated red and green light. By precisely controlling the emission wavelengths, Quantum Dots ensure that each pixel emits correct and consistent colors, resulting in vibrant and lifelike images with a wide color gamut.

## How do Quantum Dots improve color accuracy in a QD OLED monitor?

Quantum Dots in a QD OLED monitor act as precise color filters, ensuring that each pixel emits pure red, green, and blue light. This level of precision enhances color accuracy by minimizing color distortion and improving color saturation. As a result, you experience vibrant, lifelike colors with exceptional fidelity, making Quantum Dots a key part in achieving stunning visuals on a QD OLED display.

## Would a QD OLED monitor be suitable for professional photo and video editing?

Yes, a QD OLED monitor would be exceptionally suitable for professional photo and video editing. Its exceptional color accuracy, wide color gamut, and deep blacks ensure that you can accurately stand for colors and details in your work. You'll have confidence in the consistency and precision of your edits, resulting in high-quality final products.

## Does a QD OLED monitor suffer from burn-in issues like traditional OLED displays?

While burn-in can still occur on OLED displays, QD OLED monitors mitigate this risk. By integrating Quantum Dots, they distribute usage more evenly across the display, reducing the likelihood of burn-in. This technology, coupled with advancements in OLED panel design, helps minimize the risk of permanent image retention, ensuring a longer lifespan for your monitor.

## What about gaming performance on a QD OLED monitor?

Gaming on a QD OLED monitor is a delightful experience. You'll revel in smooth gameplay, thanks to the monitor's high refresh rates and fast response times. With deep black, vibrant colors, and minimal motion blur, QD OLED monitors excel at bringing your favorite games to life with stunning visuals. Get ready for an immersive gaming journey like no other!

## Could a QD OLED monitor improve my productivity?

A QD OLED monitor can significantly enhance your productivity. Its stunning visuals and exact colors make it easier to work on tasks that require precise color representation, such as graphic design or photo editing. Additionally, the clarity and detail offered by the display can reduce eye strain, allowing you to work comfortably for longer periods without fatigue.

## When can I expect QD OLED monitors to become more mainstream?

As technology advances and production costs decrease, QD OLED monitors are likely to become more mainstream within the next few years. With growing consumer demand for high-quality displays and advancements in making processes, we can expect wider adoption of QD OLED technology in various applications, making it a staple in the display market.

## Does the size of a QD OLED monitor affect its performance?

The size of a QD OLED monitor doesn't significantly change its performance. Whether you opt for a smaller or larger screen, you can expect consistent color accuracy, contrast, and brightness across the display. Factors like resolution, refresh rate, and panel quality play a more significant role in deciding overall performance, regardless of the monitor's size.

## What kind of content benefits the most from a QD OLED monitor?

Content that relies on exact color representation and high contrast benefits the most from a QD OLED monitor. This includes movies with vivid visuals, photos with true-to-life colors, graphic design work that demands precision, and gaming experiences with rich, immersive visuals. QD OLED technology ensures that each pixel emits vibrant and exact colors, enhancing the viewing experience across various types of content.

## Can I calibrate a QD OLED monitor for even better performance?

Yes, you can calibrate a QD OLED monitor to enhance its performance further. Calibration involves adjusting settings such as brightness, contrast, color temperature, and gamma to ensure exact and consistent color reproduction. Using calibration tools and software, you can fine-tune the display to meet your specific preferences or professional standards, resulting in an optimized viewing experience with improved color accuracy and clarity.

## Would a QD OLED monitor be a good choice for watching HDR content?

QD OLED monitors are an excellent choice for watching HDR content. Their high brightness, wide color gamut, and deep blacks ensure that you experience HDR content exactly as intended by content creators. You'll enjoy vibrant colors, stunning contrast, and enhanced detail, immersing yourself in the full beauty of HDR movies and shows.

## How does the refresh rate of a QD OLED monitor impact gaming?

The refresh rate of a QD OLED monitor directly affects gaming performance by deciding how smoothly and fluidly images are displayed on the screen. A higher refresh rate, typically measured in Hertz (Hz), means the monitor can refresh the image more times per second, resulting in smoother motion, reduced motion blur, and a more responsive gaming experience, especially during fast-paced action sequences.

## What should I consider when buying a QD OLED monitor?

When buying a QD OLED monitor, consider factors like screen size for your workspace, resolution for clarity, refresh rate for smoother motion, connectivity options for compatibility, and ergonomic features such as adjustable stands for comfort. Additionally, compare prices and warranties to ensure you're getting the best value for your investment. Choose a model that aligns with your specific needs and preferences.

## Can I use a QD OLED monitor for professional color-critical work?

Yes, you can confidently use a QD OLED monitor for professional color-critical work. Its exceptional color accuracy, wide color gamut, and consistent performance make it a reliable choice for tasks such as graphic design, photo editing, and video production. You can trust that your work will be displayed accurately and true to life on a QD OLED monitor.

## How does the viewing angle of a QD OLED monitor compare to other display types?

QD OLED monitors offer wide viewing angles with minimal color distortion, making them suitable for collaborative work environments or settings where viewers may not be directly in front of the screen. You can enjoy consistent image quality from various positions.

## Would a QD OLED monitor be a good choice for watching movies?

Absolutely! QD OLED monitors deliver cinematic experiences with their high contrast ratios, vibrant colors, and deep blacks. Whether you're streaming movies or watching Blu-rays, you'll appreciate the immersive visuals offered by a QD OLED display.

## Can I expect a QD OLED monitor to have built-in speakers?

While some QD OLED monitors may include built-in speakers for basic audio output, the focus is usually on the display quality rather than audio features. For a richer audio experience, you may want to invest in external speakers or a soundbar.

# What is QD-OLED? The hybrid OLED TV tech explained

We take a look at QD-OLED, a new(ish) display tech for high-end TVs

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. [Here’s how it works](https://www.techradar.com/news/about-us#section-affiliate-advertising-disclosure).

![Samsung S95C OLED TV with yellow flowers on screen](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/SQB92PZqfd4yhW3EtzA935.jpg)

TV and display tech has evolved rapidly in recent years, with manufacturers continuously improving brightness, contrast, and colour accuracy. From LCD and [OLED to QLED](https://www.techradar.com/news/oled-vs-qled), each advancement brings us closer to lifelike picture quality.

Now, a new contender has emerged: [QD-OLED](https://www.techradar.com/televisions/oled-is-dead-long-live-qd-oled), a display type that combines [OLED](https://www.techradar.com/tag/oled)’s deep blacks with quantum dot technology's vibrant colours and brightness.

But how does it work, and is it truly a game-changer?

![Two OLED TVs, one showing a cityscape, one showing a rocky landscape](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/mZSsPHqnHmyhkZ7mJtuu5S.jpg)
![Samsung S95F (left) and Sony Bravia 8 II (right) displaying an orange butterfly on screen ](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/G9bj3TSMvVChMHJZP2fiTm.jpg)
![TCL's stand at the SID show displaying its inket-printed OLEDs](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/VfNuSpDvsSCKxmcRHR3T9X.jpg)

QD-OLED, or quantum dot organic light-emitting diode (a mouthful, we know), enhances traditional OLED by using a self-emissive blue OLED light source with a quantum dot layer to generate red and green colours, aiming to improve brightness, colour purity, and efficiency over conventional OLED.

But does QD-OLED justify its premium price? How does it compare to OLED and QLED in real-world use? And is it an innovation worth investing in? We're looking at all of those questions and more to help you find the [best TV](https://www.techradar.com/news/best-tv) in 2025.

![Samsung QD-OLED display tech panel diagram](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/5Fr87G73dJaXPCP8rVXDBY.jpg)

![Samsung QD-OLED display tech panel diagram](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/5Fr87G73dJaXPCP8rVXDBY.jpg)

## What is QD-OLED? The basics

On the most basic level, QD-OLED – or quantum dot organic light-emitting diode – is a TV display tech that merges the best aspects of OLED and quantum dot LED (QLED) displays.

OLED screens use organic compounds that emit light when an electric current passes through them, allowing for self-emissive pixels that produce perfect blacks and high contrast. However, conventional OLEDs rely on a white OLED layer combined with colour filters to create red, green, and blue subpixels (and therefore images), and those filters can reduce brightness and colour efficiency.

Sign up for breaking news, reviews, opinion, top tech deals, and more.

QD-OLED takes a different approach by using a blue OLED light source with a quantum dot layer.

Quantum dots are microscopic semiconductor particles that emit highly pure colours when exposed to light. In a QD-OLED display, the blue OLED layer generates light, and the quantum dots selectively convert portions of this blue light into red and green, eliminating the need for colour filters and improving performance.

Because this process is more efficient than using colour filters, QD-OLED panels can reach higher brightness levels while maintaining excellent colour accuracy. The result is a display that combines OLED’s deep blacks and infinite contrast with quantum dots' enhanced brightness and wider colour gamut.

![Samsung S95D showing image of mountains](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/ydLWgcvLKSaPteAk8yucZW.jpg)

![Samsung S95D showing image of mountains](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/ydLWgcvLKSaPteAk8yucZW.jpg)

## QD-OLED: The pros and cons

QD-OLED introduces key improvements over [OLED and QLED displays](https://www.techradar.com/news/oled-vs-qled) – the dominant variants in most mid- and high-end TVs – enhancing brightness, colour accuracy, and viewing angles. However, as with any emerging tech, there are some drawbacks.

A major advantage of QD-OLED is its improved colour performance. By using a blue OLED light source and a quantum dot layer for red and green, QD-OLED produces purer primary colours for a more lifelike image.

Brightness and contrast are also superior to conventional OLEDs. Quantum dots are more efficient than colour filters, letting QD-OLED panels reach higher peak brightness while maintaining deep blacks, making them ideal for [HDR content](https://www.techradar.com/televisions/hdr10-vs-dolby-vision-which-hdr-format-is-best).

As if that wasn't enough, QD-OLED also offers wider viewing angles and better energy efficiency.

Unlike LED-based displays that suffer from colour shifts at an angle, QD-OLED maintains consistent image quality across a broad range. Additionally, removing colour filters reduces power consumption, making QD-OLED more efficient than traditional OLEDs.

Despite its strengths, QD-OLED is expensive. As a new tech, manufacturing costs are high right now, keeping QD-OLED TVs and monitors at premium price points. Prices should drop over time, but for now, they are likely to remain a limiting factor for most buyers.

Like all OLED-based displays, QD-OLED is still prone to burn-in. While advancements in panel longevity and pixel-shifting techniques reduce risk, prolonged display of static images can lead to image retention, making it less ideal for certain use cases like gaming, so keep that in mind.

Finally, availability is a concern. Right now only [Samsung](https://www.techradar.com/tag/samsung) Display currently produces QD-OLED panels for TVs, which is why prices for TVs that use the tech are high compared to conventional [OLED and QLED displays](https://www.techradar.com/news/best-oled-tvs-our-pick-of-the-best-oled-televisions-you-can-buy-today). As adoption grows, prices will drop, but for now, QD-OLED remains a niche option in the high-end display market.

![Sony-A95L QD-OLED TV on TV stand shown from side angle with city image onscreen](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/nMohTEJYnWViXPLaurN5pa.jpg)

![Sony-A95L QD-OLED TV on TV stand shown from side angle with city image onscreen](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/nMohTEJYnWViXPLaurN5pa.jpg)

## QD-OLED vs. OLED and QLED: What are the differences?

Broadly speaking, QD-OLED sits somewhere between OLED and QLED, offering a combination of their best traits.

Compared to standard OLED, QD-OLED delivers higher brightness and better colour accuracy. Traditional OLEDs still offer excellent contrast and deep blacks, but except for high-end models such as the [LG G4](https://www.techradar.com/televisions/lg-g4-review) that use brightness-boosting [MLA (Micro Lens Array)](https://www.techradar.com/features/micro-lens-array-and-meta-explained) tech, brightness is comparatively limited.

QD-OLED overcomes these limitations while retaining the self-emissive properties that make OLED displays superior to LED-based panels.

When compared to QLED, the biggest difference is that QD-OLED is self-emissive, meaning each pixel generates its own light, while QLED relies on an LED backlight, giving QD-OLED an edge in black levels, viewing angles, and overall picture uniformity.

However, QLED displays, especially those using [mini-LED](https://www.techradar.com/televisions/best-mini-led-tv) backlighting, can still achieve higher peak brightness and may be more affordable. Ultimately, QD-OLED offers a balance between OLED’s contrast and QLED’s brightness, making it an appealing choice for premium display buyers.

![LG G4 and Samsung S95D OLED TVs side by side displaying test pattern](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/Q4qyZUduorbFxhGELMQYAQ.jpg)

![LG G4 and Samsung S95D OLED TVs side by side displaying test pattern](https://cdn.mos.cms.futurecdn.net/Q4qyZUduorbFxhGELMQYAQ.jpg)

## Should I buy a QD-OLED TV now?

We're glad you asked! The answer is: maybe.

QD-OLED is still new, but major brands like Samsung, [Sony](https://www.techradar.com/tag/sony), and [Dell](https://www.techradar.com/tag/dell) Alienware have adopted it.

Samsung’s S95 series models like the [Samsung S95D](https://www.techradar.com/televisions/samsung-s95d-review) and [Sony’s A95 models](https://www.techradar.com/reviews/sony-a95l) showcase its strengths, while [Dell Alienware offers QD-OLED](https://www.techradar.com/computing/monitors/ive-been-in-love-with-dells-alienware-qd-oled-ultrawide-for-a-while-and-now-black-friday-has-just-slashed-its-price-to-its-lowest-ever) gaming monitors. These displays deliver excellent picture quality but remain high-end, limiting mainstream adoption.

A key challenge is pricing.

The complex manufacturing process makes QD-OLED displays more expensive than standard OLED or QLED, and while costs may drop as production scales up, affordability remains a barrier. For now, QD-OLED is positioned as a premium option rather than a mainstream choice.

Despite this, its future looks promising. Ongoing improvements in panel efficiency and durability could address burn-in concerns and enhance longevity. If more manufacturers, such as [LG](https://www.techradar.com/tag/lg) or [TCL](https://www.techradar.com/tag/tcl), enter the market, increased competition could drive down prices and improve availability.

QD-OLED has the potential to reshape display technology by combining OLED’s contrast with QLED’s brightness at a more competitive price. While currently limited to premium models, advancements in production could make it a standard for high-performance displays in the years ahead.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: QD-OLED displays utilize self-emissive technology, where each pixel generates its own light, ensuring perfect blacks and infinite contrast, enhancing overall picture quality.
Combining quantum dots with OLED, QD-OLED achieves exceptional color accuracy and brightness, producing vivid colors and deep blacks that deliver lifelike, immersive visuals.
While traditional OLEDs face burn-in issues, QD-OLED technology minimizes this risk through improved pixel distribution and usage, extending the display's lifespan and reliability.
QD-OLED redefines display technology by merging OLED's contrast with quantum dot brightness, establishing new benchmarks for color accuracy, brightness, and overall viewing experience.
